title: MCP UI DSL 1.3 — ApplicationDefinition

pages.description: Page definitions keyed by id. Routes referencing `ui://pages/<id>` resolve against this map. Pages MAY also be inlined directly inside `routes` (see RouteValue); this map is the canonical home when pages are persisted alongside the application.

description: Top-level application definition per docs/01_Core_Concepts.md §1.2 and bundle metadata per docs/11_Bundle_Metadata.md. Widget and action sub-trees are validated by widgets.schema.json (referenced via #/$defs/Widget and #/$defs/Action — left permissive here so this schema is self-contained).

Action.description: Action per docs/04_Actions.md. Discriminator `type`; per-type fields are validated by widgets.schema.json's Action def.

Alignment.description:
2D alignment within a rectangle. Token names are **directional**
(RTL-aware) per Material 3:

- `topStart` — top corner on the leading edge (top-left in LTR,
  top-right in RTL).
- `topEnd` — top corner on the trailing edge.
- `centerStart` / `centerEnd`, `bottomStart` / `bottomEnd` — same
  convention on the middle / bottom rows.
- `topCenter`, `center`, `bottomCenter` — neutral on the cross
  axis.

Object form `{x, y}` is a numeric pair where each axis runs from
-1 (start) through 0 (center) to 1 (end). The numeric form
supports any point inside or outside the rectangle.

Cross-schema canonical for any "2D rectangle alignment" use —
child placement (`box.alignment`, `align.alignment`,
`stack.alignment`), gradient endpoints (`Gradient.begin/end`),
image fit (`BackgroundImage.alignment`).

AnimationCurve.description:
Easing curve applied to an animation's progress. Eleven canonical
values cover the M3-recommended palette plus the CSS-familiar
basics:

- **CSS-familiar** — `linear`, `easeIn`, `easeOut`, `easeInOut`.
- **M3 standard** — `standard`, `standardAccelerate`,
  `standardDecelerate`. Used for everyday transitions; minimal
  overshoot, clean cubic.
- **M3 emphasized** — `emphasized`, `emphasizedAccelerate`,
  `emphasizedDecelerate`. Used for hero / page transitions; more
  dramatic acceleration profile.
- **Bounce** — `bounceIn`, `bounceOut`. For attention / alert
  micro-interactions.

Cross-schema canonical for `animatedContainer.curve`,
`animatedOpacity.curve`, RouteTransition, scrollAnimated easing,
and any future implicit-animation widget. Matches the M3 motion
spec's named tokens 1:1 so theme-level motion overrides stay
vendor-neutral.

AssetRef.description:
Reference to an asset (image, font, audio, video, SVG, …). Five
scheme prefixes are accepted, picked by the runtime to dispatch
the right loader:

- `bundle://<path>` — asset packaged inside the active bundle.
- `https://<url>` / `http://<url>` — fetched over the network and
  cached on first render.
- `data:<mime>;base64,<payload>` (or `data:<mime>,<urlencoded>`) —
  inline bytes carried in the DSL itself.
- `assets/<path>` — Flutter-style local asset path declared in the
  consumer app's pubspec asset list (used by host integrations that
  ship icon SVGs alongside the runtime).
- `client://<channel>/<id>` — resource served by the client / host
  process via an MCP client-extension channel.

Used cross-schema by `app` (splash · publisher logos · font
assets), `page`/widgets (image · icon · BackgroundImage · video
src), and `theme` (font asset registration). Lives under
`configs/_primitive/` for that reason.

blendMode.description: Curated subset of blend modes covering the common tinting use cases (`srcIn` for solid tint, `color` for hue swap, `screen`/`overlay`/`multiply` for atmosphere).

BackgroundImage.description:
Image fill behind a box's content. `image` accepts the same forms as
the `image` widget's `src` (`bundle://`, `https?://`, `data:`,
`client://`). `fit` and `alignment` mirror the `image` widget's
semantics. `opacity` applies an alpha multiplier; `colorFilter`
composites a tint via the named blend mode. `repeat` controls
tiling behaviour outside the image's natural bounds.

Binding.description:
Binding-expression string of the form `"{{...}}"`. Authors may
substitute any primitive value with a binding so the runtime
resolves the value at render time. Used as an alternative branch
in primitives that otherwise expect a specific structural shape
(AssetRef, Dimension, BorderRadius, Alignment, …).

BorderRadius.description:
Per-corner radius value. A bare number applies uniformly to all
four corners; the object form lets each corner take an independent
radius. `all` is a shorthand for setting every corner at once and
is overridden by individual corner keys.

Corner names are **directional** (RTL-aware) per Material 3:

- `topStart` — top corner on the leading edge (top-left in LTR,
  top-right in RTL).
- `topEnd` — top corner on the trailing edge.
- `bottomStart` / `bottomEnd` — same convention on the bottom row.

This is the cross-schema canonical for any "per-corner radius"
shape — referenced by `theme.Shape` (M3 shape token sizes) and by
the `BoxDecoration.borderRadius` widget property.

Category.description: Free-form. Recommended values: productivity, education, entertainment, social, business, utilities, health, finance, lifestyle, news, travel, food, sports, music, photo, video, communication, developer, reference, other.

DashboardConfig.description: Compact rendering entry point per docs/11_Bundle_Metadata.md §11.9.

Dimension.description:
Numeric size — accepts a plain `number` (logical pixels) OR the
legacy MCP UI DSL v1.0 `{value, unit}` object form. The `unit`
field is informational; the runtime treats every dimension as
logical pixels.

Gradient.description:
Multi-stop gradient fill. Selected by `type`:

- `linear` — straight axis from `begin` (default `centerLeft`) to
  `end` (default `centerRight`).
- `radial` — circular gradient out from `center` (default `center`)
  to `radius` (default `0.5`, expressed as a fraction of the
  bounding rectangle's longest side).
- `sweep` — angular gradient around `center`, swept from
  `startAngle` (default `0`) to `endAngle` (default `2π`) in
  radians.

`colors` lists at least two color values; `stops` (optional) places
each color at a position in `[0, 1]`. `tileMode` controls behaviour
past the gradient extents.

style.description: Per-item overrides layered on top of NavigationConfig.style.

indicatorColor.description: Selection indicator pill color (rail / drawer / tabs).

indicatorShape.description: Selection indicator corner radius. Defaults to a fully-rounded pill.

dividerColor.description: Drawer-only — color of dividers between item groups.

dividerIndent.description: Drawer-only — leading inset before each divider.

labelStyle.description: Default text style for item labels (selected and unselected). Per-item override via NavItem.style.labelStyle.

selectedColor.description: Foreground color (icon + label) for the selected item.

NavigationStyle.description:
Visual styling for a navigation surface (drawer / rail / tabs /
bottomBar). Every field is optional — omitted fields fall back to
the active theme's component defaults. Used by
`NavigationConfig.style` (whole surface) and `NavItem.style`
(per-item override).

Backgrounds compose: `backgroundColor` paints first, then
`backgroundImage` paints over it.

PermissionDeclaration.description: Permissions per docs/08_Client_Extensions.md §8.4. Either a flat list of permission names, a profile shorthand, or an object grouping by category.

duration.description: Total transition duration in milliseconds. Defaults to the M3 motion-system value for the chosen style.

curve.description: Easing curve for the transition's progress. Defaults to `emphasized`.

reverse.description: When true, run the transition in reverse direction (for back navigation).

RouteTransition.description:
Per-route page transition. Declared on a `RouteValue` so every
`navigate` to that route uses the same transition, OR overridden
inline on a `navigate` action. Six canonical styles:

- `slide` — incoming page slides over the outgoing along the
  direction (M3 default for forward navigation).
- `fade` — cross-fade between pages at the same position.
- `scale` — outgoing scales out, incoming scales in.
- `cube` — perspective-rotate as if turning a 3D cube; needs a
  perspective-aware compositor (falls back to `slide` when
  unavailable).
- `sharedAxis` — coordinated slide+fade across an axis (M3 motion
  pattern for sibling-level navigation).
- `fadeThrough` — outgoing fades out while incoming fades in
  after a brief zero-opacity bridge (M3 motion pattern for
  top-level navigation).

RouteValue.description:
Route target. Three accepted forms:

1. **Inline page** — full PageDefinition object embedded in the
   routes map.
2. **`ui://` URI** — string reference to a page resource served by
   the host (`ui://catalog/book/details`).
3. **Transition wrapper** — `{page, transition}` object that
   attaches a `RouteTransition` to either of the above. Use when
   a route should always animate with a specific style (e.g.,
   book chapter routes use `slide`, modal pages use
   `fadeThrough`). Authors who want to override per-call can
   still pass `transition` on the `navigate` action.

Routes.description: Map of route path (`/path`, `/users/:id`) to inline PageDefinition or `ui://` URI.

SemverLike.description: Two- or three-part version. `1.3` and `1.3.0` are both accepted; comparison uses major.minor only.

ServiceDefinition.description: Background service launched at app init per docs/01_Core_Concepts.md §1.2.1. Currently a thin shape — implementations should prefer the richer ChannelDefinition (§8.6) for stream-style services.

slots.description: Named insertion points the template body references via `{ "type": "slot", "name": "<id>" }`. See docs/09_Templates.md §9.4.

styles.description: Named style bundles the body can reference (e.g. `{{styles.container}}`). See docs/09_Templates.md §9.5.

TemplateDefinition.description: Reusable template definition per docs/09_Templates.md §9.2. The widget tree lives in `content`; the runtime registers entries by walking `application.templates` and reading `entry.content`.

TemplateLibraryRef.description: Remote template library reference per docs/09_Templates.md §9.11.1.

oneOf.description: Binding expression that resolves to a TextStyle object — typically `{{theme.typography.<role>}}`.

height.description: Line-height multiplier — `height * fontSize` is the per-line vertical advance.

shader.description: Gradient/shader fill applied to the rendered text via a shader mask. When set, `color` is ignored.

TextStyle.description:
Inline text style applied per `text` widget instance. Layered on
top of an M3 typography role when `variant` is set, or used
standalone. Mirrors the theme `TextStyle` token shape (fontFamily,
fontSize, fontWeight, lineHeight, letterSpacing, fontFeatureSettings)
and adds the per-instance fields a content app needs: color,
decoration, shader (gradient fill), and shadow stack.

Widget.description: Widget per docs/02_Widgets.md. Validated against the union in widgets.schema.json; left permissive here.
